scope.goal: Map the immediate consumers and sibling callers of the previously identified slot-table/locale conversion helpers.
scope.relationship_to_previous_milestone: This turns the 0x1407B63F4 and 0x1407B64B4 slot-table helper map into a higher-level locale/ctype consumer map. The key new evidence is that the main constructor path references the literal "bad locale name" and installs vtable-like descriptor row 0x140806A58 before calling the slot-table record constructor.

locale_name_constructor_0x1401868f0.role: Locale/ctype object construction path that resolves or builds a named locale implementation, installs a slot-table record, and updates a process-global current/default locale pointer.
locale_name_constructor_0x1401868f0.confidence: high for locale-name role due to the "bad locale name" literal and ctype/codepage call chain; medium for exact C++ class/facet naming.
observed_behavior: Initializes stack-local guard/lock records through 0x1407B6330 and 0x1407B63A8.

Reads global 0x140C558E8 as a cached locale-generation/current-id pointer and global 0x140C558D0 as a dword generation counter; when the pointer is still null, it increments 0x140C558D0 and stores the sign-extended result into 0x140C558E8.
Reads global 0x140E93700 as an existing process-global locale object pointer and can reuse it if available.
Calls 0x1401866B0 with the incoming object/context and the current generation/key, matching a table lookup by 8-byte index against a record at [RCX+0x08].
Allocates a 0x30-byte object through 0x140793B40 when no existing locale object can be reused.
Copies or builds several stack-local string/vector-like temporaries; cleanup releases non-null temporaries through 0x1407C4634.
Installs descriptor/vtable row 0x140806A58 at object+0x00, clears object+0x08, calls 0x1407B63F4 on object+0x10, then records object+0x08 as initialized.
On success, calls 0x1407B5EE8, dispatches through object slot [vtable+0x08], and stores the built/reused object into global 0x140E93700.
On missing or invalid locale name input, loads literal "bad locale name" at 0x140B6A720 and calls error helper 0x1407B57BC.

important_globals.0x140C558D0: Zero-initialized dword/qword area used as a generation counter; referenced by 0x1401868F0 and sibling locale constructor paths at 0x14047A480, 0x14047A690, and 0x1407A5CD0.
important_globals.0x140C558E8: Zero-initialized qword area used by 0x1401868F0 as a cached generation/current-id pointer.
important_globals.0x140E93700: Virtual-only .data global current/default locale object pointer; loaded near function entry and stored on successful construction.

important_calls.0x1407B6098: Consumes a source locale name/string into stack-local temporaries before object construction.
important_calls.0x1407B6104: Finalizes or normalizes the temporary name bundle before cleanup.
important_calls.0x1407B6330: Small lock/guard helper used around locale globals.
important_calls.0x1407B63A8: Slot/default helper used to release or leave the guard state.
important_calls.0x140793B40: Generic allocator used for the 0x30-byte locale object allocation.
important_calls.0x1407B57BC: Error throw/report helper used with literal "bad locale name".

single_byte_ctype_transform_loop_0x14018.role: Inline loop fragment that applies 0x1407B64B4 to each byte in a caller-owned buffer.
single_byte_ctype_transform_loop_0x14018.confidence: high for byte transform loop; medium for whether this is tolower/toupper/facet-specific dispatch without the full parent function.
observed_behavior: Saves RSI, sets RSI to RCX+0x10, then loops from RBX to RDI.
For each byte at [RBX], calls 0x1407B64B4 with ECX=byte and RDX=RCX+0x10 slot-table record.
Stores the returned AL back to [RBX] and increments RBX until it reaches RDI.
single_byte_ctype_transform_loop_0x14018.note: The lack of direct callers and use of live RBX/RDI indicates this pdata range is a continuation/cold fragment of a larger ctype/facet method rather than a standalone public entrypoint.

sibling_codepoint_conversion_helper_0x14.role: Uppercase-oriented sibling of 0x1407B64B4 using the same slot-table record layout and conversion wrapper.
sibling_codepoint_conversion_helper_0x14.confidence: medium-high for uppercase conversion semantics due to the ASCII fallback 0x61..0x7A subtracting 0x20.
observed_behavior: Mirrors 0x1407B64B4 argument handling: ECX is the codepoint/index and RDX is an optional record pointer.
When no record is provided, pulls default context from 0x1407D37D0 and default codepage/int from 0x1407D3770.
When no conversion context string/table exists, applies ASCII lowercase-to-uppercase fallback for 0x61..0x7A by subtracting 0x20.
For codepoints below 0x100, validates through 0x1407C46F0 or record table bit tests, then uses 16-bit table entries to choose one-byte or two-byte intermediate encoding.
Calls 0x1407B7BC8 with EDX=0x200, output buffer at [RSP+0x70], and stack argument 0x03; returns one output byte or a two-byte combined value.
sibling_codepoint_conversion_helper_0x14.relationship_to_0x1407b64b4: 0x1407B64B4 has uppercase-to-lowercase ASCII fallback and calls 0x1407B7BC8 with EDX=0x100; this sibling has lowercase-to-uppercase fallback and calls 0x1407B7BC8 with EDX=0x200.

multibyte_conversion_length_or_copy_help.role: Locale-aware multibyte conversion helper that either falls back to a raw span copy/count or calls 0x1407B7BC8 one or two times.
multibyte_conversion_length_or_copy_help.confidence: medium-high for conversion-length/copy wrapper shape; medium for exact standard-library method name.
observed_behavior: Computes source/output span lengths from R9-R8 and RDX-RCX.
If the optional record argument is null, pulls default record/string state from 0x1407D37D0 and 0x1407D37A0; otherwise reads [record+0x08] and [record+0x00].
When both conversion context and codepage/int are zero, falls back to 0x1407B9C90 over the raw spans and returns the copied/count result.
Otherwise calls 0x1407B7BC8 with EDX=0x400 and no caller output pointer to measure/convert into a temporary path.
If the first conversion result is positive and does not exceed the available source/output span, calls 0x1407B7BC8 again with the caller output pointer and length argument.
Returns the final conversion count/result in RAX/RDI.

descriptor_0x140806a58.role: Primary vtable/descriptor row installed by 0x1401868F0 and 0x140186720.
descriptor_0x140806a58.note: Provider-descriptor heuristics do not classify this as an existing provider descriptor pattern, but qword readback clearly shows a vtable-like executable slot row.

wrapper_0x140186b30.role: Wrapper around 0x1401868F0 that dispatches through object/vtable callbacks before and after locale construction.
observed_behavior: Calls qword ptr [RAX+0x08] before invoking 0x1401868F0.
Calls qword ptr [R8+0x40], qword ptr [RDX+0x10], and qword ptr [R8] around optional object/facet callbacks.
Returns a boolean-like byte result in EAX.
Scans an input zero-terminated byte string for length.
Uses stack small-string style storage, 0x1400B3520, and 0x1400B32D0 to build/copy a string object.
Validates heap small-string release boundaries before calling 0x1407B8478 or 0x1407BF200.
Installs descriptor/vtable row 0x140806B98 into [RBX].
constructor_0x140186ba0.descriptor_0x140806b98_note: The row begins with executable slots but also includes UTF-16-like qwords spelling an api-ms-win-core-sync string after slot 10, so it should be treated as an adjacent descriptor/resource row until its exact layout is mapped.

decoder_gaps_closed: Decoded opcode 0x98/0x99 sign-extension forms, including CDQE and CQO.
Corrected FF /0 and FF /1 INC/DEC operand sizing so non-REX.W forms decode as 32-bit operands, while REX.W forms remain 64-bit.
tooling_updates.why_it_matters: The main locale constructor window now decodes with zero unknown instructions and accurately reports the generation-counter path as INC EAX followed by CDQE, rather than splitting CDQE into DB bytes or widening the counter increment incorrectly.

validation_notes.qword_warning_explanation: Expected for virtual-only .data readback at 0x140E93700 and 0x140E57B40; address references remain valid.
validation_notes.literal_anchor: 0x140186B06 references ascii literal "bad locale name" at 0x140B6A720.

next_targets: Trace the four direct callers of 0x1401868F0: 0x140186B30, 0x1404790D0, 0x140479660, and 0x1407A5CD0, prioritizing the shared references to globals 0x140C558D0/0x140C558E8/0x140E93700.
Map 0x1407B6098, 0x1407B6104, 0x1407B5EE8, and 0x1407B57BC enough to distinguish locale-name parsing, temporary cleanup, object activation, and bad-locale error reporting.
Trace 0x1407A8C5E and 0x1407A8D80 because they call or neighbor the 0x1407B78E0 multibyte conversion helper and also reference the "bad locale name" literal.
Expand descriptor row 0x140806A58 into named slots by exporting 0x140186830, 0x140186890, 0x1401868C0, 0x140186B20, 0x140187A40, 0x140478F20, and 0x140478270.
Map the allocator/release helpers 0x140793B40, 0x1407C4634, and 0x1407B8478 across locale object construction so object lifetime is tied to concrete heap ownership.
